_meta.description: Sword of Vermilion script VM opcode table (VM-001)
_meta.note: Bytes $00-$DD and $E0-$F6 are display characters (tile = byte + 0x04C0). Bytes $F7-$FF and $DE-$DF are control codes.

opcodes.description: End of dialogue. Sets Script_text_complete = FLAG_TRUE. Clears Script_reading_player_name and Script_source_offset if in name-reading mode.
Advance Script_output_y by 2 (double-height text rows); reset Script_output_x to 0.
Draw continuation arrow glyph (tile $04DF) at column 20, row 27 in the dialog box. Set Script_has_continuation = FLAG_TRUE. Pause script until player presses a button.
Store operand byte as Dialog_response_index. Set Script_has_yes_no_question = FLAG_TRUE. No glyph rendered; execution pauses for player reply.
Show a YES/NO question. Store response_index, trigger_offset and ext_trigger. Set Dialogue_event_trigger_flag and Script_has_yes_no_question.
Quest choice with expected answer. Sets Quest_choice_pending = FLAG_TRUE. Also sets Dialog_response_index from byte[1].

operands.name: count
operands.description: Number of action entries that follow

operands.name: entries
operands.description: Each entry is one of: [$00,offset.b]=set trigger; [$01,sector.b]=reveal map sector; [$02,bcd0,bcd1,bcd2,bcd3]=add kim reward

entry_types.action: Add BCD kim reward: Transaction_amount/item_id/quantity/flags from bytes 2-5; call AddPaymentAmount

opcodes.description: Execute a list of actions: set event flags, reveal map sectors, or give kims. Plays SOUND_ATTACK after all actions. Marks Script_text_complete.

operands.name: count
operands.description: Number of 1-byte trigger offsets that follow

operands.name: offsets
operands.description: Each byte is an offset into Event_triggers_start; that byte is set to 0xFF

opcodes.description: Set one or more event trigger flags (simplified form of ACTIONS). Low-byte offsets only ($00-$FF range). Marks Script_text_complete.
Switch script source to Player_name buffer. Characters are read from Player_name[Player_name_index] until the name ends, then resume from original script pointer.
Wide character high surrogate byte. Renders glyph at position (cursor_x - 1, cursor_y - 1) — one tile to the upper-left of the current cursor. Used for 2-column-wide kanji-style characters.
Wide character low surrogate byte. Same rendering path as WIDE_HI (both use ScriptRender_PortraitTile). Treated identically at the dispatch level.

Display character. VRAM tile = byte + 0x04C0, palette bit set, merged with Script_tile_attrs. Written to Plane B tilemap at (Script_output_base_x + Script_output_x, Script_output_y). Advances Script_output_x by 1.
Display character (gap between WIDE range and control codes). Same rendering path as $00-$DD.
